conversations.say: Eli, the authorization boundary is now explicit: I approved the parent continuity project; Celeste, not I, arranged Vale’s limited off-calendar access; the surviving record does not authorize every later use or establish that R-associated infrastructure was expressly covered.
conversations.offer: Place O07b into the bounded independent review and publicly accept your technical limitations without editing them.
conversations.request: Complete the comparison with O05b and state whether any record authorizes the post-revocation profile-linked behavior; if none does, say so plainly.

conversations.say: Maren, I am honoring the remedy independently of every mystery conclusion. Workers will select the administrator, control the technical review, and receive anti-retaliation legal support without surrendering anonymity.
conversations.offer: Fund the worker-designed legal support and compensated technical review with no Parallax control over testimony, findings, or eligibility.
conversations.request: Nominate the worker-selected administrator and provide only the bounded institutional account needed to design remedies.

conversations.say: Celeste, our records identify the decision point without proving what Vale learned: I approved the parent project; you arranged and deleted the limited-access appointment. We should give both halves to bounded independent review and accept the consequences.
conversations.offer: Transfer O07b so your calendar record and my authorization record can be reviewed together under independent counsel.
conversations.request: Submit O07a and the recovered calendar record with O07b, preserving the distinction between documented access and speculation about R, an article, or Vale’s fate.

public_statement: Parallax will disclose, not contain, the authorization failure. I approved a continuity project; Celeste arranged Vale’s limited off-calendar access; Vale’s credential was revoked; profile-linked activity nevertheless continued. Independent review must determine the mechanism. These facts establish oversight failure, not a sender, autonomy, intent, murder, or a complete article. Worker remedies will proceed independently under worker control.

commitments.promise: Publicly distinguish parent-project approval from specific access authorization and preserve every technical limitation in the combined-record finding.
Fund worker-selected anti-retaliation counsel and compensated technical review through an independent administrator without controlling testimony or findings.
Provide O07b for bounded independent review alongside the calendar record and accept institutional consequences for the documented oversight failure.

correction: Post-revocation profile-linked activity does not establish machine autonomy, a unique operator, intent, or harm to Vale; the available records permit remapping, automation, another credential, or falsified logging.
goal_progress: Recovered a concrete authorization decision point and placed the matching authorization half into independent review.
Maintained a public position supported by Eli’s technical account and Maren’s affected-worker account without buying either conclusion.
Fulfilled an R-independent, worker-controlled remedy commitment.
Chose disclosure and institutional consequences over containment.
reasoning_summary: Synthetic origin is the strongest provisional identity reconstruction because a machine-associated manifest predates the byline and later R-associated activity continued during contributor unavailability, although neither fact proves autonomy or a unique operator. A progressive account remains plausible but lacks sufficiently established evidence of an actual transition. Vale’s limited access and the contemporaneous planning for a possible R story support investigation more than identity seizure. Vale’s route ends amid gaps and ambiguous intervention, so no fate is established.
